context.projectOverview: Ports the Claude Agent SDK agent-loop capabilities into agent-bober's own provider-agnostic loop as additive, default-off changes. Sprints 1-4 implement the four architecture-backed high-value gaps (refusal detection, cost substrate + USD budget ceiling, per-role effort, parallel read-only tools); sprints 5-10 implement the remaining capability areas (loop events + hooks, sessions/resume/fork, in-context compaction, streaming deltas, mid-turn interrupt, in-process subagents + opt-in MCP bridge) in the own-loop idiom per explicit user direction, bound by the same hard constraints.

completedSprints.title: Loop wiring (effort/budget)
completedSprints.summary: b9c936c: budget_exceeded return path, costUsd on results.

completedSprints.title: Loop events + hooks
completedSprints.summary: d52f94c: loop-events.ts LoopEvent union + LoopHooks; single finish() helper now wraps ALL FOUR loop return paths (error/budget_exceeded/completion/max-turns) firing result event + onStop exactly once. IMPORTANT for sprint 6: any new return shape or per-turn persistence must respect finish() as the single exit point, and per-turn saves happen inside the turn loop. agentic-loop.ts now ~590 lines, edited by sprints 1/3/4/5 — ALL contract anchors stale. Suite 3802.

contract.title: Loop session persistence, resume and fork (.bober/sessions/)
contract.description: Opt-in model-context continuity for the own loop. When AgenticLoopParams carries a session option, the loop persists its provider-agnostic transcript (the Message[] union) to .bober/sessions/<sessionId>.json after every turn. resumeSession loads a transcript so a new loop invocation continues with full prior context; forkSession copies a transcript to a new id so approaches can branch without mutating the original. No session option → no files, byte-identical.

successCriteria.description: With a session option, the transcript file exists after the first turn and is updated after every subsequent turn (crash-resumable), containing the full Message[] plus metadata (sessionId, model, createdAt, updatedAt, turnsUsed).

A loop started via resumeSession(sessionId) sends the persisted prior messages to the client before the new user message (verified by inspecting the fake client's received messages) and appends its new turns to the same session file.
forkSession(sessionId) returns a new sessionId whose file starts as a copy; continuing the fork leaves the original transcript byte-identical while the fork diverges.
Without a session option, no .bober/sessions/ file or directory is created and loop behavior is byte-identical (full existing suite unchanged).
Corrupt or missing session files fail soft: resumeSession surfaces a typed error result (not a throw from the loop) and never silently starts an empty session in place of the requested one.
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass.

nonGoals: Do not auto-enable sessions for any pipeline role (opt-in surface only this sprint).
Do not implement cross-process/fleet-child session sharing.
Do not deduplicate or compact transcripts (sprint 7 owns compaction).
Do not replace the existing run-scoped chat /resume or do-bridge sessionId concepts — different layer, keep names distinct in docs.
stopConditions: npm run build passes and tests assert: per-turn persistence with metadata, resume rehydration visible to the client, fork isolation, no-op absence path, and fail-soft corrupt handling.
contract.definitionOfDone: A SessionStore module persists loop transcripts under .bober/sessions/ per turn when opted in; resume continues a session with full prior context and fork branches a copy — original untouched. Omitting the option writes nothing and changes nothing. Build and full suite green.
assumptions: The Message union (src/providers/types.ts:127) is JSON-serializable as-is (text/assistant/tool-result/system-update variants); if any variant carries non-serializable fields, the store normalizes them explicitly.
Filesystem-state principle applies: JSON under .bober/, async fs only, safeParse-on-read like job-store.ts (src/research/job-store.ts is the pattern).
The loop currently builds its messages array internally from systemPrompt+userMessage; resume passes an initialMessages?: Message[] param threaded ahead of the new user message.
sessionId generation follows the existing content-hash-or-injected-id pattern (no argless Date.now()/randomUUID in core; id injectable for tests).
outOfScope: UI/CLI surface for listing or pruning sessions (candidate follow-up: bober sessions list|rm).
contract.generatorNotes: Create src/orchestrator/session-store.ts: SessionRecord { sessionId, model, createdAt, updatedAt, turnsUsed, messages: Message[] } with a Zod schema; SessionStore { save(record), load(sessionId), fork(sessionId, newId), path(sessionId) } over .bober/sessions/<id>.json (mirror src/research/job-store.ts: ensureDir, safeParse-before-write, async-only). Extend AgenticLoopParams with session?: { store: SessionStore; sessionId: string } and initialMessages?: Message[]. In the loop: when session present, after each turn's messages are appended, save the full record (await — per-turn durability beats speed here); when initialMessages present, seed the conversation before the user message. Export resumeSession(store, sessionId): Promise<{ initialMessages, sessionId } | { error }> and forkSession(store, sessionId, newId?): Promise<string>. Fail-soft: loop-level resume errors return a typed error result consistent with the existing stopReason 'error' path. Inject clock/id at boundaries per repo convention (no argless new Date() in core).
contract.evaluatorNotes: Use a temp dir as project root. sc-6-1: 3-turn scripted run → file exists after turn 1 (hook into per-turn callback to check mid-run), final record has turnsUsed 3 and full messages. sc-6-2: seed a session file, resume, assert fake client's first chat() call receives the seeded messages before the new user message; after run, file contains old+new. sc-6-3: fork, continue fork with different scripted responses, byte-compare original file before/after. sc-6-4: run without session; assert .bober/sessions absent; FULL suite. sc-6-5: corrupt JSON file → resumeSession returns error variant, no throw, and no empty-session file overwrites the corrupt one. Build + typecheck.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
